data.title: Rudding Park partners with Agilysys for tech upgrade; Agilysys to modernize operations in luxury resort.
data.description: In London and Alpharetta, Georgia, Agilysys, Inc. (Nasdaq: AGYS), a prominent provider of hospitality technology solutions globally, has shared news today. Rudding Park, an acclaimed hotel, spa, and golf resort in Harrogate, has selected Agilysys' hospitality software solutions to facilitate a significant technology overhaul. This move is part of Rudding Park's expansion into a multi-amenity luxury hotel business. Over the span of almost 20 years, Rudding Park has transitioned from a single hotel into a complex luxury destination.

data.title: OBUSPA integrated with prpl and RDK, reducing service deployment time from months to weeks; prplWare and RDK-B involved.
data.description: A significant advancement has taken place in Fremont, California, as broadband service providers (BSPs) utilizing prpl Foundation and RDK platforms can now roll out new services much faster. Thanks to integrations with the Broadband Forum’s OBUSPA open source software, deployment time has been reduced from months to just weeks. This breakthrough impacts two popular carrier-grade open source platforms for home broadband management: prpl Foundation’s prplWare and RDK’s RDK-B software. The new integration allows for quicker service implementation and is poised to transform the broadband industry.

data.title: Kaldalón hf. to auction 6-month bills, KALD 26 0901 series, in ISK 20 million units on 24 Feb, managed by Landsbankinn hf. Payment/settlement on 2 March 2025.
data.description: Kaldalón hf. is set to conduct an auction on 24 February for a new series of six-month bills, titled KALD 26 0901 bills. The company is committed to regular issuance of bills and bonds, with the auction employing the Dutch method. Each bill will have a nominal value of ISK 20 million, and Kaldalón hf. retains the discretion to fully accept, reject, or partially accept bids. Landsbankinn hf. will oversee the auction process. Interested investors must submit bids detailing the nominal amount and flat yield to Landsbankinn hf. at verdbrefamidlun@landsbankinn.is by 16:00 on 24 February. The results and allocation of bills will be announced before the market opens on 25 February. Payment and settlement for successful bids are scheduled for 2 March 2025.

data.title: Eviden and Almaviva partner to secure deployments of Cooperative Intelligent Transport Systems in Italy.
data.description: Eviden and Almaviva have recently partnered in Italy to ensure the safe deployment of Cooperative Intelligent Transport Systems. This collaboration aims to enhance road safety and efficiency by integrating advanced technologies. With Eviden's expertise in system security and Almaviva's experience in transport systems, they plan to implement cutting-edge solutions across the country. The collaboration is set to benefit a significant number of commuters and motorists, improving overall transportation infrastructure.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs witness $133M outflows amid ‘extreme fear’ sentiment. US spot Bitcoin ETFs record $238M outflow this week, marking a five-week streak since March 2025.
data.description: Spot Bitcoin ETFs listed in the US have experienced a decrease of $238 million in assets this week, marking the first five-week period of outflows since March 2025.

data.title: Cristiano Ronaldo boosts Herbalife shares by 14% after acquiring 10% stake in Pro2col Software for $7.5 million.
data.description: Shares of Herbalife Ltd. (NYSE: HLF) surged by 14.27% in after-hours trading to reach $18.90, following a 3.76% increase during the regular session, closing at $16.54. The global health and wellness company announced that soccer star Cristiano Ronaldo acquired a 10% stake in HBL Pro2col Software LLC, a subsidiary of Herbalife, for $7.5 million post-market on Wednesday. The investment includes services and sponsorship rights for Pro2col Software, Herbalife's advanced AI-powered health and wellness platform. Ronaldo's involvement aims to enhance the data-driven capabilities of the platform. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ICANN CEO discusses 2026 domain expansion opportunities during visit to Turkey. President and CEO Kurtis Lindqvist concludes a two-day visit.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, in Istanbul, the President and CEO of the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN), Kurtis Lindqvist, completed a two-day visit to the Republic of... The global Internet community is gearing up for the upcoming expansion of domain names in 2026.

data.title: Lario Therapeutics gets $2.4m to expand calcium channel platform for CNS disorders, including Parkinson's Disease and PTSD.
data.description: Lario has recently obtained prestigious grants to enhance their calcium channel programs, expanding into Parkinson's disease and PTSD. This development builds upon past endorsements from The Michael J. Fox Foundation, reinforcing Lario's precision neuroscience endeavors.

data.title: Prinoth debuts PrinothView, digital shield for hazard detection, along with new products at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2026. ST. PETER, Minn.
data.description: Prinoth has integrated hazard detection technology into its new remote-controlled Raptor 60 and compact Grizzly M300e mulcher, along with the Prinoth Connect fleet management platform. The announcement was made in St. Peter, Minnesota on February 19, 2026. Prinoth, known for its Jarraff by Prinoth tree care equipment, continues to innovate with these new additions.

data.title: Amsterdam, Berlin, and Frankfurt named top destinations for Brits to relocate to in Europe.
data.description: A recent study conducted by digital expat insurer Feather has unveiled the most popular European destinations for a hassle-free relocation. Amsterdam, Berlin, and Frankfurt have emerged as the top choices for individuals looking to make a simple move, as per the research findings released on February 19, 2026. The report highlights a growing trend of people opting to relocate, with an increasing number showing interest in moving to these cities.

data.title: Everbridge hosting London event on incident management at Churchill War Rooms for IT, DevOps, and operational leaders.
data.description: A company known as Everbridge, Inc., a prominent player in High Velocity Critical Event Management (CEM) and national public warning solutions, is scheduled to organize a customer event on March 17 at the Churchill War Rooms in London. This event aims to gather IT, DevOps, and operational leaders to converse about the changing requirements of current incident management and operational resilience. Themed as “The Future of Incident Management: A Blueprint for Operational Excellence,” the event will include discussions on strategies and best practices for successful incident management.
